what_it_does: Vitest test suite that exercises discovery, parsing, and SQLite persistence of OpenAI Codex CLI rollout transcripts alongside Claude transcripts.
observations: Fixtures simulate Codex JSONL rollouts with session_meta, turn_context, response_items, reasoning blocks, function calls, and local shell calls.
Tests assert getConversationSourceDirs returns both Claude projects and Codex sessions directories.

parseConversation is expected to produce a ConversationExchange with harness='codex', project derived from cwd, sessionId, gitBranch, agentVersion, model, modelProvider, user/assistant messages, and paired toolCalls.
Local shell calls are paired with their outputs by call_id.
A database test inserts an exchange and asserts harness/session/agent/model/model_provider columns.
Environment variables CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, CODEX_HOME, TEST_PROJECTS_DIR, and TEST_DB_PATH are sandboxed in beforeEach/afterEach.
A comment notes the live embedding axis is 2560 INT8 versus a retired 384 dimension.
standouts: Dual-harness source directory discovery test (Claude + Codex).
Codex-specific JSONL fixture covering reasoning, exec_command, and local_shell_call types.
Database schema assertion for harness and model metadata persistence.
conventions: Defines reusable fixture helper functions for JSONL lines.
Restores environment variables in afterEach to avoid test pollution.
Uses toMatchObject and expect.objectContaining for partial shape assertions.
concerns: Vector dimension 2560 is hardcoded in the test with only a comment explaining it.
Project name is inferred from a hardcoded cwd path in fixtures, so the test does not exercise real path resolution.
parseConversation is called with the rollout path twice and a hardcoded year string, making the signature intent unclear from this file alone.
notes: Test file that codifies how Codex rollout transcripts are discovered, parsed into exchanges and tool calls, and persisted with harness metadata.
questions: How does parseConversation derive the project name from the cwd path?
How are multiple exchanges split from a single Codex rollout JSONL file?
What is the full exchanges table schema beyond the selected metadata columns?
Does the local_shell_call_output format always include the 'Exit code: ...\nOutput: ...' prefix?
purpose_vs_actual: No explicit purpose statement in the file; actual content matches a test suite validating Codex transcript ingestion support.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish and lock in first-class support for OpenAI Codex CLI transcripts within the same indexing pipeline used for Claude transcripts, ensuring parsing, tool-call pairing, and schema storage all capture Codex-specific metadata.
novelty: It tests a second AI harness (Codex) in the same indexing pipeline, including Codex-specific local shell call types and harness metadata fields.
contribution: Provides concrete Codex JSONL fixtures and assertions that define the expected shape of parsed exchanges, tool-call pairing, and persisted harness metadata for Codex transcripts.
summary_for_embedding: Vitest test suite for parsing and persisting OpenAI Codex CLI rollout transcripts into the recollection indexing pipeline alongside Claude transcripts.
intention_for_embedding: To verify that Codex transcript discovery, exchange extraction, tool-call pairing, and harness metadata storage work correctly.

symbols.does: Writes an array of objects to a file as JSON Lines.
symbols.description: A small test helper that serializes each element of the input array to JSON and writes the resulting newline-delimited content to the specified path with a trailing newline.

symbols.does: Returns a fixture array representing a Codex rollout JSONL with session metadata, turn context, user message, reasoning, exec_command call, output, and assistant reply.
symbols.description: This fixture constructs a realistic Codex transcript sequence used by tests to verify that parseConversation extracts harness metadata, user and assistant messages, and paired function-call/tool-result records.

symbols.does: Returns a fixture array representing a Codex rollout JSONL that uses local_shell_call and local_shell_call_output items.
symbols.description: This fixture is used to test pairing of Codex-specific local shell invocations with their outputs, ensuring the parser recognizes call_id-based tool-result matching for the local_shell_call type.
